factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: DXY hovering around 99.10 with modest losses; US-Iran peace hopes cited as dampener. No directional conviction; need next USD data to clarify.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: No explicit TIPS or real yield data in news; cannot assess primary gold driver. This is a critical gap for gold analysis.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran-US Tensions)
factors.summary: US-Iran negotiations in 'final stages' per Trump (FXStreet 23:33); oil record drawdown at Goldman Sachs pace (Bloomberg 03:06); Strait of Hormuz threats. Safe-haven bid present but negotiations may ease tension.

factors.name: Fed Policy & Rate Expectations
factors.summary: No new FOMC decision or Powell speak in this window. ECB June hike nearly certain per Reuters sources; no direct US hawkish surprise to support DXY or hurt gold.

factors.name: Central Bank Policy (BoJ)
factors.summary: BoJ board member Koeda warns inflation near 2% and may exceed target; war-led inflation overshoot risk cited. Suggests BoJ rate hikes likely June (Carlyle, Bloomberg 01:46), tightening bias, historically bearish gold via stronger yen and higher real yields.

factors.name: Oil Prices (WTI/Brent)
factors.summary: WTI stabilizing above $98 after ~5% drop; Goldman reports record oil stockpile drawdown but prices recovering. Lower oil reduces inflation urgency, may ease 'war premium' and reduce safe-haven demand.

factors.name: Global Inflation Data
factors.summary: Japan flash PMI shows record-high selling prices (19-year high) but services stalled; core CPI forecast to ease to 1.7% (below BoJ 2% target) per Investinglive. Conflicting signals on price pressures.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment & Equity Action
factors.summary: Nvidia record profit, $80bn buyback; Samsung strike deal averted; SoftBank +20%; KOSPI sidecar halted. Strong risk-on, which can compete with safe-haven gold bid.

factors.name: Safe-Haven Flows
factors.summary: Gold holding near $4,550 'as market eyes Middle East'; no exceptional haven flows. Silver building on bounce from $73 lows (FXStreet 02:20). Steady but not explosive.

factors.name: 24h Price Action
factors.summary: Gold +1.70% in 24h but only -0.15% in 4h. Move extended in upward direction; suggests Phase 2 rally may be maturing, not fresh opportunity.

screening.narrative: Gold has rallied +1.70% over 24h on geopolitical safe-haven demand (US-Iran tensions, oil stockpile drawdown) and mixed inflation signals (Japan PMI selling prices at 19-year highs vs. slowing CPI). However, the move is extended on the 4h (only -0.15% in last 4h suggests momentum waning), and risk sentiment remains strong (equity rally on Nvidia/Samsung/SoftBank). The BoJ's hawkish tone (Koeda warning of inflation overshoot, rate hike expected June) weighs on gold via implied higher real yields and yen strength. Without clear data on US real yields or a fresh geopolitical shock, the rally lacks conviction.
catalysts_ahead: Japan April CPI data (due May 21 ~23:30 GMT, per Investinglive) — core forecast 1.7% YoY, below BoJ 2% target; outcome may trim BoJ rate-hike odds or reaffirm them
BoJ June rate hike probability (market pricing) — if odds rise, bearish for gold via real yields
US-Iran negotiations resolution (Trump said 'final stages') — peace deal = risk-on, hurt gold; escalation = safe-haven, support gold
Oil inventory/production data — record drawdown narrative may reverse if supply eases
ECB June 11 rate decision (nearly certain hike per Reuters) — may indirectly lift global real yields, pressure gold
US economic calendar: any CPI, PCE, or NFP print in next 72h would reset tone
Equity earnings momentum (Nvidia, Samsung deals set tone) — sustained risk-on may cap safe-haven demand
screening.risk_warning: Main invalidators: (1) Fresh escalation in Iran-US conflict or Strait of Hormuz threat → flight to safety, gold rallies further; (2) Surprise dovish BoJ signals or weak Japan CPI → real yields fall, gold rallies; (3) Major US inflation print (CPI/PCE hot) → real yields rise, DXY surges, gold sold; (4) Equity correction / VIX spike → safe-haven bid returns. Current read is neutral because DXY, real yields (unknown), and inflation narrative are all unresolved.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is extended in 24h context (+1.70% from lower levels); 4h momentum faded (-0.15%). Wait for pullback to $4,500–$4,520 support to test bullish continuation, OR await Japan CPI data for directional clarity. Avoid chasing into resistance $4,560–$4,575 without new catalyst confirmation.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: USD hovering near 99.10, supported by US-Iran diplomatic uncertainty and hawkish Fed pricing. DXY strength is directly headwind for gold.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: Treasury yields resuming climb amid inflation concerns (per FXStreet 07:06), but real yields unclear from current data. Nominal yields up on inflation fears is mixed signal for gold.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations & Data
factors.summary: Mixed signals: inflation fears rippling through markets (FXStreet 07:29), but no fresh CPI/PCE data in this window. BoJ's Koeda notes inflationary risk materializing, ECB signaling potential rate hikes (07:05). This supports USD/real yields, headwind for gold.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran War)
factors.summary: Trump signaled US-Iran talks in 'final stages,' sparking risk-on rotation yesterday. Oil prices edging lower (~$98.20 WTI, ~$106 Brent), reducing inflation urgency. Peace hopes undercut safe-haven bid.

factors.name: Oil Prices & Energy Shock
factors.summary: Oil retreating on Iran deal optimism (WTI below $98.50), reducing immediate inflation driver. Goldman Sachs notes oil stockpiles falling at record pace (03:26), but lower spot prices reduce gold's inflation hedge benefit.

factors.name: Global Growth Data
factors.summary: France PMI collapsed to 43.5 (07:15, steepest decline in 5.5 years), Germany PMI 48.6 contracted second month (07:30). Eurozone weakness could soften growth expectations, but combined with ECB hawkish tilt, real yields supported.

factors.name: Central Bank Policy Tilt
factors.summary: ECB Rehn (05:08) signals 'adverse scenario' may warrant rate hikes for credibility. BoJ Koeda (03:59, 05:43) emphasizes inflation risk is materializing; some time before June meeting but hawkish bias evident. Higher rates globally are headwind for gold.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows & Physical Demand
factors.summary: No data in news window on GLD/IAU holdings or physical demand shifts. Indian rupee stabilizing post-RBI intervention (03:30+), suggesting RBI may be less compelled to buy gold as emergency reserve support.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment & Safe-Haven Demand
factors.summary: Mixed: equities rebounding on Iran peace hopes and AI strength (SoftBank +20%, Nvidia results). Asian stocks higher (Japan +3.1%, Australia +1.47%), reducing immediate flight-to-safety bid for gold.

factors.name: Silver (Proxy for Risk Asset Behavior)
factors.summary: XAG/USD down 0.7% to $75.20 (06:17), facing pressure as US yields rebound. Silver's weakness suggests industrial/risk asset headwinds, consistent with gold pressure.

screening.narrative: Gold has retreated -0.53% in both 4h and 24h windows on a combination of strengthening USD (DXY ~99.10), rising real yields driven by hawkish central bank commentary (ECB, BoJ), and easing geopolitical premium as Trump signaled final-stage Iran negotiations. Oil prices sliding below $100 on deal optimism has removed immediate inflation urgency, undercutting gold's hedge appeal. Weak European PMI data (France composite 43.5, steepest in 5.5 years) has not sparked a gold rally as might be expected; instead, it has combined with ECB tightening signals to support real rates. Risk-on sentiment (equities rallying on AI strength, lower oil) is crowding out safe-haven demand. The move is modest and in early phase, but momentum is down.
catalysts_ahead: US CPI / Core CPI release (if scheduled within 24-72h) — hot data would extend gold weakness via real yields
ECB June policy meeting (looming) — Rehn's 'adverse scenario' signals possible hike, BULLISH for EUR, BEARISH for gold
BoJ June policy meeting — Koeda's hawkish inflation talk suggests potential adjustment coming
Iran-US diplomatic updates — further peace progress would extend risk-on, weigh on safe-haven bid
US Treasury yield curve moves — any sharp rise in nominal yields would pressure gold despite growth concerns
Key US economic data (retail sales, ISM Services PMI) — if weak, could trigger real-yield reversal
screening.risk_warning: If Iran talks collapse or Middle East escalates sharply, geopolitical bid could snap back and reverse gold weakness instantly. Conversely, if US inflation data surprises hot in next 48h, nominal yields could spike further and drag gold lower despite safe-haven demand. Fed hawkishness priced into USD is fragile — any dovish pivot from Powell or other Fed speakers would flip the bias to BULLISH for gold. Equity rally is momentum-driven on AI hype; if sentiment cracks on earnings disappointments, flight-to-safety could reverse the current headwinds.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early Phase 2 downtrend (-0.53% intraday); avoid chasing shorts into $4,510 support. Better risk/reward on LONG entries if gold retests $4,500–$4,510, OR on confirmation that real yields and DXY have peaked (watch for ECB/BoJ news to stabilize). Short bias only if DXY breaks above 99.50 AND 10y nominal yield closes above prior resistance (need specific level data to confirm).

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: USD gave back early gains as Treasury yields corrected sharply on renewed US-Iran peace deal optimism. DXY weakness is structurally bullish for gold, but momentum is fading.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: Yields corrected lower on geopolitical de-escalation hopes, but FXStreet notes inflation expectations (5y5y swaps) are catching up with Europe amid prolonged disruption risks. Conflicting signals—no clear directional thrust.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (US-Iran)
factors.summary: Iran reviewing US peace proposal; Pakistan army chief visiting Tehran to mediate. Market pricing significant de-escalation scenario, reducing safe-haven premium for commodities and supporting risk-on rotation away from gold.

factors.name: Oil Prices & Inflation Expectations
factors.summary: Brent fell sharply on Iran deal hopes despite Strait of Hormuz supplies 95% below normal. Oil weakness should ease inflation fears, reducing Fed hawkishness and pressuring gold's inflation hedge narrative.

factors.name: UK/Eurozone Economic Data
factors.summary: UK Services PMI collapsed to 47.9 vs 51.7 expected; Composite PMI 48.5 (weakest in 13 months); France private sector at 5+ year low. Broadening economic weakness could trigger safe-haven bid, offsetting geopolitical relief.

factors.name: Fed Policy & Rate Path
factors.summary: No recent FOMC decision or Powell speak in news window. Market awaiting May PMI data (due today) and next inflation prints. Insufficient clarity on forward rate cuts vs hawkish hold.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows & Physical Demand
factors.summary: No GLD/IAU flow data in news window. No seasonal demand catalyst (not wedding/CNY season). Structural CB buying not mentioned today.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment & Equity Action
factors.summary: Equities rebounding (Nvidia earnings drew muted reaction but KOSPI/Nikkei sharply higher); tech rebound extends. Risk-on flows typically compete with safe-haven gold demand.

factors.name: Competing Safe Havens
factors.summary: USD weakness vs gold (typically positive), but JPY also benefiting from risk-on fade. CHF not mentioned. No clear flow advantage for gold over alternatives.

factors.name: Price Action Context
factors.summary: 4h down 0.36%, 24h down 0.48%. Small declines but directionally consistent with geopolitical relief (sell-off in safe havens). Move is incremental—not extended yet.

screening.narrative: Gold is in a modest decline (-0.48% in 24h) as markets price US-Iran peace deal hopes, reducing geopolitical safe-haven premium. Dollar weakness and correcting yields should be structurally bullish, but oil's sharp fall on de-escalation optimism and broadening Eurozone/UK economic weakness have created cross-currents. Risk sentiment is turning modestly risk-on (tech rebounding, equities rising), which competes with gold demand. Without a fresh inflation surprise, Fed pivot signal, or geopolitical escalation, gold lacks a clear directional conviction.
catalysts_ahead: US S&P Global Flash PMI (May) — released later today; key to growth vs inflation narrative
US Memorial Day holiday Friday — reduced trading liquidity
ECB/BoE economic calendar (limited data expected before next policy meetings)
Iran-US negotiations update — any escalation risk would reverse gold's downside
Fed commentary if any officials speak (none scheduled in immediate window)
screening.risk_warning: If US-Iran peace agreement is formally announced, gold could extend declines to $4,450–$4,480 as risk-on rally accelerates and real yields stay anchored. Conversely, if Eurozone/UK recession fears deepen or PMI data shocks lower, flight-to-safety flows could quickly restore a bid to $4,550+. Current neutrality is vulnerable to binary outcomes.
screening.entry_guidance: Avoid entries at current price; move is choppy on competing cross-currents. If gold breaks below $4,500, consider short entry with stop at $4,540 on further de-escalation confirmation. If it rallies back to $4,540, look for rejection into long entry with stop below $4,480 if economic data disappoints later today.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: US bond yields have surged sharply on Iran war inflation fears; nominal 10y Treasury yields rising as markets price in Fed tightening (per Bloomberg 'Bond Yield Surge' coverage). Higher real yields are directly bearish for gold.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: USD strengthening as higher yields attract carry-seeking capital (per MUFG commentary on 'Fed shift and AI-driven risk backdrop'). Stronger dollar inversely pressures gold; EUR/USD and Sterling both down ~0.9% since mid-May.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran/Hormuz)
factors.summary: Ongoing Iran conflict, Strait of Hormuz closure, uranium enrichment disputes, and toll-system talks create significant safe-haven demand. Oil +3% on Supreme Leader uranium statement; physical Hormuz supply 95% below normal per MarketWatch. Gold typically benefits from macro uncertainty and flight-to-safety.

factors.name: Oil Prices (WTI/Brent)
factors.summary: Oil surged >3% on Iran uranium news and tight supply via closed Hormuz. Higher energy prices feed inflation expectations, which can trigger fed-hedging demand for commodities and inflation-linked assets like gold.

factors.name: Fed Policy Outlook
factors.summary: FOMC minutes signaled gradual hawkish shift; market now pricing multiple rate hikes due to inflation shock from Iran war (per MUFG and Bloomberg). Hawkish pivot weighs on nominal and real yields, pressuring gold in near term.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations & PMI
factors.summary: US May flash PMI at 51.7 (confirmed, prior 51.7); services PMI 50.9 vs 51.1 expected—subdued growth but S&P Global notes 'price surge' from war impact. Input cost pressures rising (Kansas Fed: 'raw materials prices remained elevated'). Inflation is creeping up, supporting Fed tightening bias.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment & Equities
factors.summary: S&P 500, Nasdaq slipping amid higher yields and oil; tech sector weakness (Nvidia -1.86%, Intel -3.57% per Bloomberg). VIX likely elevated but equities remain near record highs—not yet a full risk-off scenario. Mixed sentiment limits safe-haven rally.

factors.name: Price Action: 24h Change
factors.summary: Gold down -0.76% in 24h; 4h change minimal at +0.01%. No sustained buying momentum despite geopolitical headlines. Move is lateral, suggesting buyers are not stepping in decisively.

factors.name: Central Bank Flows & Positioning
factors.summary: No fresh CB gold-buying news in the past 24h. Structural CB bid remains, but not a near-term driver of current price action.

factors.name: Competing Safe Havens
factors.summary: USD and US Treasuries are winning the safe-haven race due to yield pickup. JPY intervention threat keeps USD/JPY capped below 160, supporting dollar flow into gold-competitive assets.

screening.narrative: Gold is caught in a cross-current: geopolitical tensions (Iran, Hormuz) and rising oil prices support a safe-haven narrative, yet surging US Treasury yields and a stronger US Dollar (reflecting Fed tightening expectations in response to war-driven inflation) are pressuring real yields and making USD-denominated gold more expensive globally. The 24h decline of -0.76% and flat 4h action signal that despite headlines, institutional buyers are not yet committing to a gold surge; instead, capital is flowing into higher-yielding US assets and the US Dollar. The battle between safe-haven demand (bullish) and real-yield pressure (bearish) remains unresolved.
catalysts_ahead: PCE and Core PCE inflation prints (timing TBD, likely week of May 27–June 3)
FOMC June meeting expectations (any hawkish surprise will pressure gold further)
Iran-US peace negotiations and Hormuz toll system resolution (ongoing, real-time)
Oil inventory reports and supply flow updates (weekly)
Fed speakers (Waller, Williams, Barkin, etc.) on inflation and rate path
Equity earnings revisions and market breadth (AI narrative dominance)
USD/JPY intervention signal or BoJ policy moves
screening.risk_warning: If Federal Reserve signals a slower hiking pace, or if inflation data cools unexpectedly, real yields could fall sharply, triggering a powerful gold rally. Conversely, if geopolitical tensions de-escalate (Iran deal or Hormuz toll agreement), safe-haven demand evaporates and gold could re-test $4,450–$4,400. Also monitor: a sharp equity selloff (VIX spike >25) would flip sentiment back to gold as a hedge.
screening.entry_guidance: Bias is NEUTRAL-to-BEARISH in near term; gold likely retest $4,480 support before any bullish reversal. If geopolitical headlines ease, expect a move lower to $4,420–$4,450. Do not chase the 0.76% 24h decline—wait for either a convincing 4h close below $4,480 (for a short entry) or a true risk-off event (equities crash, VIX >30, real yields plunge) to justify a bullish entry.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: US Treasury yields spiking sharply (10y now ~4.6%, approaching 5%) due to Iran war-induced oil shock and inflation concerns. Higher nominal yields without clear deflation signal = higher real yields, directly negative for gold.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: Strong USD flows driven by geopolitical premium (safe-haven), higher yields, and manufacturing strength (PMI strong per multiple reports). Stronger DXY is structurally bearish for XAUUSD.

factors.name: Oil Prices & Geopolitical Risk
factors.summary: WTI >$101 and spiking on Hormuz closure fears and US-Iran standoff. Bloomberg reports elevated energy prices may force central banks to keep rates higher longer. Inflation expectations rising sharply—intermediate bearish for gold, as higher real yields dominate.

factors.name: Fed Policy Stance
factors.summary: Fed speakers (Goolsbee, Barkin) flagging 'significant inflation problem' and no rate-cut bias. Policy is 'in a good place to respond to shocks' but no dovish pivot. Hawkish tone supports higher yields, negative for gold.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations
factors.summary: Iran war stoking supply-side inflation fears; energy prices elevated, mortgage rates jumping to 6.5%+ (8-month high per MarketWatch). Market pricing in persistent inflation, not deflation. Banxico minutes cite 'upside inflation risks' from Middle East conflict.

factors.name: US Economic Data
factors.summary: Kansas Fed Manufacturing index +9 (solid), US Manufacturing PMI strong. Labor market stable per Barkin. No recession signal yet, which removes some safe-haven demand for gold.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows & Central Banks
factors.summary: No recent flow data in this window. Canada RBC notes higher gold prices helping exports, but no evidence of major institutional buying or PBoC accumulation reported.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment & Safe-Haven Demand
factors.summary: Equity market spiking on Iran deal hopes (stocks up after 'draft agreement' report); bond market showing stress (credit-equity divergence per MarketWatch). Conflicting signals—gold hasn't rallied despite geopolitical risk, suggesting risk-on dominates near-term.

factors.name: Technical: Price Action
factors.summary: 4h change +0.85%, 24h change +0.19%. Minimal momentum; move is weak and fragmented. Consolidating in a narrow range after prior NEUTRAL reads (07:50, 11:21, 15:21 all NEUTRAL 5/10).

factors.name: Iran Deal Uncertainty
factors.summary: Conflicting reports: initial 'draft deal reached' headlines buoyed risk appetite, but uranium demands and Hormuz toll system remain unresolved per Al Jazeera and Secretary Rubio. Uncertainty is keeping yields elevated and DXY firm; resolution would likely push oil lower and relieve inflation fears, benefiting gold longer-term.

screening.narrative: Gold is caught in a powerful headwind from surging real yields (10y Treasuries approaching 5% on Iran oil shock) and a strong US Dollar bid driven by geopolitical safe-haven flows and higher rates. While initial Iran deal optimism fueled a 0.85% 4h rally, the underlying fundamentals—persistent inflation fears, hawkish Fed messaging, and elevated energy prices—continue to pressure gold. The market is pricing in a 'higher for longer' rates regime rather than the deflation or dovish pivot that would be needed to sustain a gold rally. Without a resolution to the Iran-Hormuz standoff or a sharp inflation surprise to the downside, gold remains structurally pressured.
catalysts_ahead: US-Iran peace deal announcement or failure (hours–next 48h)
Oil price reaction to geopolitical developments (ongoing, real-time)
Fed speakers (Goolsbee, Barkin, Powell if scheduled) on inflation/rates
US economic data (weekly jobless claims, any revisions to manufacturing/services PMI)
Treasury yield action if 10y breaks above 5% or retraces sharply
Next CPI/PCE print (date TBD in 1-2 weeks) — critical for inflation narrative reset
screening.risk_warning: Risk to this NEUTRAL/bearish read: (1) US-Iran deal breakthrough causing oil to plunge and real yields to compress rapidly (bullish gold); (2) Unexpected Fed dovish pivot or economic data miss (e.g., NFP weakness) prompting yield repricing lower; (3) VIX spike and equities selloff if geopolitical talks collapse, forcing safe-haven flow into gold despite higher yields; (4) Technical bounce if gold closes above $4,570 on two consecutive closes could signal a shift to BULLISH bias.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early Phase 2 with weak momentum (+0.85% in 4h). Short-term: avoid chasing the rally. If gold bounces to $4,560–$4,580, monitor for rejection and consider positioning for a retest of support ($4,520) or larger structural short if yields hold above 4.5%. If Iran deal collapses and oil spikes above $105, reassess—real yield compression could flip bias to BUY on pullback to $4,500. Currently: wait for either yield retracement or geopolitical clarification before adding conviction.

factors.name: US-Iran Deal Optimism
factors.summary: Draft US-Iran agreement reportedly finalized with Pakistani mediation; oil prices collapsed from $98+ to $96.35 (-1.94%), reducing inflation tail risk and supporting safer assets. FXStreet reported gold recovering mid-North American session on this geopolitical relief.

factors.name: Oil Price Decline
factors.summary: WTI crude down 1.94% on deal hopes removes immediate energy inflation shock (Strait of Hormuz closure risk fades). Lower energy input inflation is dovish for Fed hold duration, supporting real yields and gold inversely.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: No explicit TIPS data in news, but nominal 10y Treasury rallied ~10bps on risk-off reversal (from 4.6% threat back lower per mortgage rate reports). Real yields likely steady to slightly lower; minimal directional clarity without fresh TIPS print.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: No explicit DXY move reported. USD/CAD trimmed gains as oil fell; broader dollar context unclear. Geopolitical de-escalation typically weakens safe-haven USD flow, mildly supportive for gold.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations & Fed Guidance
factors.summary: Fed's Barkin struck hawkish-neutral tone ('current policy is in good place,' not leaning toward rate cuts); mortgage rates surged to 6.51% (highest since Aug), signaling sticky inflation concerns. Bloomberg noted gold declined on 'persistent concerns that elevated energy prices may force central banks to keep rates higher for longer.'

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk Premium
factors.summary: US-Iran draft agreement removes immediate escalation risk; Hormuz closure threat (which Rapidan warned could rival 2008 recession) now much lower. Safe-haven demand for gold softens but fundamental conflict resolution is dovish.

factors.name: Price Action Context
factors.summary: Gold +0.44% in 4h and only +0.02% in 24h—minimal momentum. Move is early-stage reaction to deal news, not yet extended; supports Phase 2 designation.

factors.name: Competing Safe Havens & Flows
factors.summary: No GLD/IAU ETF flow data reported; no explicit central bank gold buying news in window. BoJ yen carry unwind risk absent from headlines.

screening.narrative: Gold caught a bid this afternoon on hopes of a US-Iran deal finalization, which sent oil prices sharply lower (-1.94% to $96.35). The geopolitical de-escalation removes the near-term inflation tail risk (Hormuz closure scenario) and sparked a risk-on bounce, reversing earlier heavy yields and equities. However, Fed speakers (Barkin) remain hawkish-neutral, mortgage rates remain stubbornly elevated at 6.51%, and Bloomberg's commentary emphasized that sticky energy inflation could keep rates elevated longer—a headwind for gold's real-yield tailwind. The move is fresh (Phase 2) but modest in absolute terms, suggesting market is still parsing conflicting signals: relief on Iran offset by Fed hawkishness.
catalysts_ahead: US-Iran deal official announcement (expected 'within hours' per ILNA/Al-Arabiya as of 19:30 UTC)
Mortgage rate follow-through (if 10y Treasury holds gains, re-test of $4,560 likely; if Treasuries reverse, gold falters)
Additional Fed speakers (Barkin done; others may comment on inflation/rate path)
Oil price stability test (if WTI holds <$97, further inflation relief; if bounces back, re-ignites rate-hold narrative)
No major US data (NFP next Friday, no CPI/PCE in next 48h)
screening.risk_warning: If Iran deal falls through or announcement is delayed/fails (as political chop suggested in afternoon volatility), oil rebounds sharply and gold gives back gains. Alternatively, if Fed officials pivot more hawkish in coming 24h or Treasury yields reverse higher on sticky inflation concerns, the real-yield bid for gold evaporates and the move invalidates. Watch 10y yield hold below 4.55% as confirmation; break above 4.65% invalidates bullish bias.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is underway but modest (+0.44% in 4h). Wait for retest of $4,520–$4,530 support or Treasuries to confirm <4.50% before adding longs; alternatively, short-term traders can enter 4h break above $4,560 with tight stop at $4,545 if Iran deal confirmed solid. Avoid chasing here—phase 2 positioning.

factors.summary: No fresh CPI/PCE data in news window. Mortgage rates hit highest since August (6.51% per Bloomberg 18:18), suggesting inflation concerns persist, but no new inflation surprise to anchor bias. Mixed messaging from Walmart warning on consumer spending vs. Workday AI strength.

factors.summary: Bloomberg headline (18:50 UTC) notes AI may be worsening inflation picture. Mortgage rates spiking. War-related energy supply risks (Hormuz closure, Iran conflict) still present structural inflation risk, but near-term de-escalation hopes are tempering this.

factors.summary: US equities ended +0.55% (Dow), Canada +0.72% on peace deal optimism (20:31 UTC). Not risk-off equity collapse; rather cautious risk-on. Gold's safe-haven bid competing with appetite for risk assets.

factors.summary: No new PBoC, RBI, or other CB gold purchase data in news window. Structural bid still intact but no fresh catalyst.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows
factors.summary: No GLD/IAU holdings or flow data provided in news window.

factors.name: Economic Data Quality
factors.summary: US Manufacturing PMI beat expectations (20:27 UTC), supporting cautious Fed hold; Japanese CPI due Friday 22 May (expected below BOJ 2% target per investinglive RSS). No major US economic surprise to anchor gold directionally.

screening.narrative: Gold rallied modestly (+0.09% 24h) on fresh US-Iran peace deal headlines and geopolitical risk premium, with safe-haven flows offsetting weaker oil prices. The draft agreement narrative (reported ~20:19 UTC) triggered a burst of risk-off appetite into equities and treasuries, but Iranian officials quickly contradicted the deal-done narrative, creating chop. Real yields remain the key determinant but lack specific 10y TIPS data in this news cycle; nominal yields compressed slightly on safe-haven bid. The move is still early-phase but conflicting geopolitical messaging and modest price action (+0.20% in 4h, +0.09% 24h) suggest either a nascent Phase 2 or continued chop into the weekend.
catalysts_ahead: Japan CPI Friday 22 May 2026 — third consecutive month below BOJ 2% target would soften global yield expectations
ECB Speaker Philip Lane at Asian Monetary Policy Forum Friday (Singapore) — Europe and World Economy remarks could shift expectations on ECB rate path
US-Iran deal finalization or collapse — high volatility if announced; impacts oil, risk sentiment, and inflation expectations
Major US data releases (NFP, CPI, PCE, ISM) — no specific dates given but Q2 print cycle approaching; early June likely
screening.risk_warning: If US-Iran deal is formally announced and oil prices stabilize lower (~$90-95), inflation fears recede sharply, real yields rise, and DXY strengthens, gold could see a corrective pullback toward $4,480–$4,500. Conversely, if geopolitical tensions re-escalate or oil spikes back above $100, the bid could rapidly resume. The conflicting Iran narrative in the past 2 hours shows headline risk is elevated; avoid chasing the move higher without fresh catalyst confirmation.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early Phase 2; wait for a pullback to $4,520–$4,525 support before adding long. If deal narrative collapses (oil spiking back), reassess; if confirmed and oil stable, gold likely consolidates $4,500–$4,560 into Friday data releases. Do not chase above $4,560 without clear break of resistance and real-yield compression.

factors.name: US-Iran Draft Agreement
factors.summary: Al Arabiya reported a final draft US-Iran agreement reached via Pakistani mediator (19:02 UTC). Oil fell 1.94% to $96.35, indicating reduced inflation risk and lower real-yield floor, directly supportive for gold.

factors.name: Real Yields / TIPS
factors.summary: No explicit TIPS yield data in news, but Treasuries erased earlier drops and notched gains on Iran peace signals (18:49 UTC), suggesting yields declining. Lower real yields are the single strongest structural gold bullish driver.

factors.name: Oil Price Direction
factors.summary: WTI crude settled -1.94% at $96.35 on US-Iran deal hopes. Lower oil = lower inflation expectations = lower Fed hold duration = lower real yields = gold upside.

factors.name: USD Index (DXY)
factors.summary: DXY trades neutral near 99.20 on strong US Manufacturing PMI, but peace deal narrative typically weakens USD safe-haven bid. No explicit USD weakness yet, but geopolitical de-escalation historically pressures DXY.

factors.name: Fed Policy Stance
factors.summary: Fed's Barkin (19:40 UTC) eschews forward guidance, citing uncertainty on inflation, employment, and AI labor impacts. No rate-cut signals; policy remains on hold, which is mildly supportive for gold but not a primary driver.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran-US)
factors.summary: Peace deal momentum reduces tail risk of Strait of Hormuz closure and sustained high oil/inflation. De-escalation typically benefits risk assets and reduces gold volatility floor, but structural bid from inflation hedging remains intact.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment
factors.summary: Asian equities set for second day of gains on Iran optimism (Bloomberg 22:06 UTC); no explicit VIX or equity crash signals. Risk-on environment can compete with gold safe-haven flows but also reduces crisis hedging premium.

factors.name: Central Bank Gold Buying
factors.summary: No fresh CB buying data in this news cycle. Structural PBoC/RBI bid remains in background but not activated by fresh headlines.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations
factors.summary: No US CPI/PCE or breakeven data in news window. Japan CPI on agenda for May 22 (20:37 UTC), but US inflation expectations remain anchored absent new surprises.

factors.name: Price Action Extension
factors.summary: 24h change only +0.07% and 4h change +0.21% — move is nascent. However, prior three reads (17:56, 19:53, 21:26 UTC on May 21) all showed BUY with increasing confidence, suggesting a fresh Phase 2 rally attempt underway.

screening.narrative: Gold is staging a cautious recovery on US-Iran peace deal drafting, which has triggered oil selloff (-1.94%) and brief Treasury rallies, signaling lower real yields ahead. The dominant macro story is geopolitical de-escalation reducing inflation tail risk and allowing the Fed to maintain a patient, non-hawkish stance. However, the move remains early-stage (only +0.21% in 4h, +0.07% in 24h) and sentiment hinges on deal confirmation—Iran's President said on state TV 'they won't back down,' introducing headline noise. Absence of fresh US inflation data or Fed guidance keeps conviction at moderate levels.
catalysts_ahead: Japan CPI May 22 (early Tokyo session) — misses could reinforce BOJ patience narrative and weaken JPY, supporting gold
ECB's Philip Lane speaks May 22 at Asian Monetary Policy Forum — any hint of lower-for-longer could steepen long-end yields, pressuring gold
US-Iran deal announcement/denial — critical binary; any deal collapse would send oil and yields back higher
Fed speakers (Barkin already spoke, but Waller/Williams may chime in) — current narrative is 'cautious on both sides', so hawkish surprise would be tail risk
Broader macro data calendar (NFP, ISM, retail sales) not imminent in next 24–48h, limiting fresh economic shocks
screening.risk_warning: Deal announcement reversal or collapse would invalidate the peace-rally thesis and trigger risk-off, likely crushing oil and equities but also spiking yields and USD, which would hurt gold despite flight-to-safety. Alternatively, a surprise hot US inflation print (especially if PMI or credit market stress resurfaces per JPMorgan's 'termites' narrative) would flip real-yield dynamics sharply bearish and trigger a sharp DXY spike, crushing gold.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early Phase 2; pullback to $4,520–$4,530 support would offer better risk/reward than current levels. Avoid chasing the open; wait for retest of support with volume to confirm before adding longs.
